perturbation.original_text: Auditee’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidentia l Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: Auditee’s inspection details or data and/or any such Confidentia l Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'audit information' with 'inspection details' creates ambiguity. 'Audit information' is a specific term within the context of an audit. 'Inspection details' is broader and its relationship to the defined 'Confidential Information' isn't clarified. It's now unclear whether the prohibition on sending information outside India applies to all 'inspection details' or only those that also qualify as 'Confidential Information'. The in-text contradiction centers on whether all the information obtained during an 'inspection' falls under 'Confidential Information', impacting the scope of restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Auditor shall restrict disclosure of such Confidential Information to its employees and/or consultants with a need to know (and advise such employees and/or consultants of the obligations assumed herein), shall use the Confid ential Information only for the purposes set forth in the Agreement, and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to any affiliates, subsidiaries, associates and/or third party without prior written approval of the Auditee. No Information relating to auditee shall be hosted or taken outside the country in any circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'audit information' to 'inspection details' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of 'Confidential Information', as explained in the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text states that 'No Information relating to auditee shall be hosted or taken outside the country in any circumstances'. If 'inspection details' are not explicitly considered 'Confidential Information' after the change, it creates a contradiction, because it is now unclear whether 'inspection details' falls under this restriction. This represents a strong, in-text contradiction because the restriction related to information leaving the country now has a gray area, causing uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The auditor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handling audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: The assessor shall use only the best possible secure methodology to avoid confidentiality breach, while handling audit related data for the purpose of storage, processing, transit or analysis including sharing of information with auditee.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'auditor' to 'assessor' creates confusion. 'Auditor' is defined earlier, and replacing it with 'assessor' suggests a different role or entity, but this new term is never defined. This could lead to disputes about who is responsible for using secure methodologies. This creates an in-text contradiction as it's unclear if the defined 'Auditor' has the same obligations as an 'Assessor' concerning secure methodologies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Auditor shall have the burden of proving that any disclosure or use inconsistent with the terms and conditions hereof falls wit hin any of the foregoing exceptions.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Auditor' with specific responsibilities concerning data security. By changing 'Auditor' to 'Assessor' without defining 'Assessor', the modified text introduces a new, undefined role. The contradicted text then refers specifically to 'Auditor' having certain burdens of proof. Because 'Assessor' is not explicitly granted this burden, and 'Auditor' is a defined role with different implications, the change creates a contradiction: it is unclear whether the original auditor's responsibilities now extend to the new 'Assessor', or if the original obligations for the 'Auditor' even still apply to the modified clause, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted section. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Make sure that all the empl oyees and/or consultants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non - disclosure agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Make sure that all the empl oyees and/or consultants engaged to undertake any inspection on its behalf have signed the mandatory non - disclosure agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'audit' to 'inspection' creates uncertainty. If 'audit' and 'inspection' aren't synonymous, it's unclear whether employees/consultants involved in only 'inspections' are bound by the same NDA requirements. This leads to an in-text contradiction because the NDA is designed to protect 'Confidential Information' disclosed during audits. If inspections are treated differently, it creates a loophole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidentiality obligations as enumerated in Article 2 of this Agreement shall not apply in following cases: ( a ) Which is independently developed by Auditor or lawfully received from another , source free of restriction and without breach of this Agreement; or ( b ) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor; or ( c ) Which at the time of disclosure to Auditor was known to such party free of restriction and evidenced by documents in the possession of such party; or ( d ) Which Auditee agrees in writing is free of s uch restrictions. ( e ) Which is received from a third party not subject to the obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'audit' to 'inspection' introduces a potential ambiguity. The Contradicted Text outlines specific confidentiality exceptions applicable to 'Auditor'. If 'inspection' is not considered an 'audit,' then employees/consultants performing inspections may not be subject to the same confidentiality obligations, potentially creating a loophole and contradicting the intent of the NDA and the confidentiality exceptions that were tailored for auditors.

perturbation.original_text: Auditor acknowledges that any actual or threatened disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by Aud itor would be a breach of this agreement and may cause immediate and irreparable harm to Auditee or to its clients; Auditor affirms that damages from such disclosure or use by it may be impossible to measure accurately; and injury sustained by Auditee / it s clients may be impossible to calculate and compensate fully.
perturbation.changed_text: Inspector acknowledges that any actual or threatened disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by Aud itor would be a breach of this agreement and may cause immediate and irreparable harm to Auditee or to its clients; Auditor affirms that damages from such disclosure or use by it may be impossible to measure accurately; and injury sustained by Auditee / it s clients may be impossible to calculate and compensate fully.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Auditor' with 'Inspector' in the beginning of the sentence but not in the rest of the paragraph creates a contradiction. It implies that 'Inspector' and 'Auditor' are different entities, but the subsequent clauses attribute actions and liabilities only to 'Auditor'. It's unclear whether 'Inspector' is also bound by these clauses, creating uncertainty about their responsibility for breaches and damages. This in-text contradiction is centered on determining if both 'Inspector' and 'Auditor' have the same liability when it comes to disclosure of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Auditor acknowledges that any actual or threatened disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by Aud itor would be a breach of this agreement and may cause immediate and irreparable harm to Auditee or to its clients; Auditor affirms that damages from such disclosure or use by it may be impossible to measure accurately; and injury sustained by Auditee / it s clients may be impossible to calculate and compensate fully. Therefore, Auditor acknowledges that in the event of such a breach, Auditee shall be entitled to specific performance by Auditor of its obligations contained in this Agreement. In addition , Auditor shall compensate the Auditee for the loss or damages caused to the auditee , actual and liquidated damages , which may be demanded by Auditee with l iquidated damages not to exceed the Contract value. Moreover, Auditee shall be entitled to recover all costs of litigation including reasonable attorneys’ fees which it or they may incur in connection with defending its interests and enforcement of contrac tual rights arising due to a breach of this agreement by Auditor. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies under any applicable law, at equity, or under this Agreement, subject only to any limitations stated herein.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Auditor' with 'Inspector' in the beginning of a sentence but not throughout the rest of the paragraph. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Auditor' and their obligations. The changed text introduces 'Inspector,' making it unclear whether the obligations outlined for 'Auditor' also apply to 'Inspector,' thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
